qa_pairs.question: Which clusters currently have the AmberTools 21 module available?
qa_pairs.answer: The AmberTools 21 module is currently available on all clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What molecular dynamics (MD) engines are provided by AmberTools 21?
qa_pairs.answer: AmberTools 21 provides the following MD engines: `sander`, `sander.LES`, `sander.LES.MPI`, `sander.MPI`, `sander.OMP`, `sander.quick.cuda`, and `sander.quick.cuda.MPI`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I set up the AMBER environment variables after loading the AmberTools 21 module?
qa_pairs.answer: After loading the AmberTools 21 module, you need to source the environment variables by running: `source $EBROOTAMBERTOOLS/amber.sh`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two versions of `amber/20` modules available?
qa_pairs.answer: There are two versions of `amber/20` modules: `20.9-20.15` and `20.12-20.15`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the key differences between `amber/20.9-20.15` and `amber/20.12-20.15`?
qa_pairs.answer: The `amber/20.9-20.15` module uses MKL and `cuda/11.0`, while `amber/20.12-20.15` uses FlexiBLAS and `cuda/11.4`.

qa_pairs.question: Why is FlexiBLAS used in some `amber/20` modules instead of MKL?
qa_pairs.answer: MKL libraries do not perform well on AMD CPUs. FlexiBLAS addresses this by detecting the CPU type and using libraries optimized for the specific hardware.

qa_pairs.question: Which CUDA version is required for running Amber simulations on A100 GPUs on Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: CUDA version `11.4` is required for running Amber simulations on A100 GPUs installed on Narval.

qa_pairs.question: What MD programs are included in the CPU-only `Amber/20` modules?
qa_pairs.answer: CPU-only `Amber/20` modules provide all MD programs available in AmberTools/20, plus `pmemd` (serial) and `pmemd.MPI` (parallel).

qa_pairs.question: What additional programs do the GPU modules for `Amber/20` provide?
qa_pairs.answer: GPU modules for `Amber/20` add `pmemd.cuda` (for single GPU) and `pmemd.cuda.MPI` (for multi-GPU).

qa_pairs.question: Which modules should I load to run `ambertools/21` on CPUs using `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: To run `ambertools/21` on CPUs with `StdEnv/2020`, load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 openmpi/4.0.3 scipy-stack ambertools/21`.

qa_pairs.question: Which modules should I load to run `ambertools/21` on GPUs using `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: To run `ambertools/21` on GPUs with `StdEnv/2020`, load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 cuda/11.4 openmpi/4.0.3 scipy-stack ambertools/21`.

qa_pairs.question: What notes are associated with `ambertools/21` when used with `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: The `ambertools/21` module used with `StdEnv/2020` includes GCC, FlexiBLAS, and FFTW.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are required for `amber/20.12-20.15` CPU runs under `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/20.12-20.15` CPU runs under `StdEnv/2020`, you need to load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 openmpi/4.0.3 amber/20.12-20.15`.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are required for `amber/20.12-20.15` GPU runs under `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/20.12-20.15` GPU runs under `StdEnv/2020`, you need to load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 cuda/11.4 openmpi/4.0.3 amber/20.12-20.15`.

qa_pairs.question: What notes are associated with `amber/20.12-20.15` when used with `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: The `amber/20.12-20.15` module used with `StdEnv/2020` includes GCC, FlexiBLAS, and FFTW.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are required for `amber/20.9-20.15` CPU runs under `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/20.9-20.15` CPU runs under `StdEnv/2020`, you need to load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 openmpi/4.0.3 amber/20.9-20.15`.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are required for `amber/20.9-20.15` GPU runs under `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/20.9-20.15` GPU runs under `StdEnv/2020`, you need to load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 cuda/11.0 openmpi/4.0.3 amber/20.9-20.15`.

qa_pairs.question: What notes are associated with `amber/20.9-20.15` when used with `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: The `amber/20.9-20.15` module used with `StdEnv/2020` includes GCC, MKL, and FFTW.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed for `amber/18.14-18.17` CPU runs using `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: To run `amber/18.14-18.17` on CPUs using `StdEnv/2020`, you need to load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 openmpi/4.0.3 amber/18.14-18.17`.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are listed for `amber/18.14-18.17` GPU runs using `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/18.14-18.17` GPU runs using `StdEnv/2020`, the listed modules are `StdEnv/2020 gcc/8.4.0 cuda/10.2 openmpi/4.0.3`. The `amber/18.14-18.17` module itself is not explicitly listed for GPU in this entry.

qa_pairs.question: What notes are associated with `amber/18.14-18.17` when used with `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: The `amber/18.14-18.17` module used with `StdEnv/2020` includes GCC and MKL.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed for `amber/18` CPU runs using `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: To run `amber/18` on CPUs using `StdEnv/2016`, you need to load `StdEnv/2016 gcc/5.4.0 openmpi/2.1.1 scipy-stack/2019a amber/18`.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed for `amber/18` GPU runs using `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: To run `amber/18` on GPUs using `StdEnv/2016`, you need to load `StdEnv/2016 gcc/5.4.0 openmpi/2.1.1 cuda/9.0.176 scipy-stack/2019a amber/18`.

qa_pairs.question: What notes are associated with `amber/18` when used with `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: The `amber/18` module used with `StdEnv/2016` includes GCC and MKL.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed for `amber/18.10-18.11` CPU runs with `gcc/5.4.0` under `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/18.10-18.11` CPU runs with `gcc/5.4.0` under `StdEnv/2016`, you need to load `StdEnv/2016 gcc/5.4.0 openmpi/2.1.1 scipy-stack/2019a amber/18.10-18.11`.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed for `amber/18.10-18.11` GPU runs with `gcc/5.4.0` under `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/18.10-18.11` GPU runs with `gcc/5.4.0` under `StdEnv/2016`, you need to load `StdEnv/2016 gcc/5.4.0 openmpi/2.1.1 cuda/9.0.176 scipy-stack/2019a amber/18.10-18.11`.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed for `amber/18.10-18.11` CPU runs with `gcc/7.3.0` under `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/18.10-18.11` CPU runs with `gcc/7.3.0` under `StdEnv/2016`, you need to load `StdEnv/2016 gcc/7.3.0 openmpi/3.1.2 scipy-stack/2019a amber/18.10-18.11`.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed for `amber/18.10-18.11` GPU runs with `gcc/7.3.0` under `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: For `amber/18.10-18.11` GPU runs with `gcc/7.3.0` under `StdEnv/2016`, you need to load `StdEnv/2016 gcc/7.3.0 cuda/9.2.148 openmpi/3.1.2 scipy-stack/2019a amber/18.10-18.11`.

qa_pairs.question: What notes are associated with `amber/18.10-18.11` when used with `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: Both `amber/18.10-18.11` configurations under `StdEnv/2016` include GCC and MKL.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed for `amber/16` CPU runs under `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: To run `amber/16` on CPUs under `StdEnv/2016`, you need to load `StdEnv/2016.4 amber/16`.

qa_pairs.question: Is `amber/16` available for GPU runs under `StdEnv/2016`?
qa_pairs.answer: `amber/16` is not listed with any specific modules for GPU runs under `StdEnv/2016` in the provided table.

qa_pairs.question: On which cluster is `amber/16` available, and what are its limitations?
qa_pairs.answer: `amber/16` is available only on Graham, and some Python functionality is not supported.
